title: Integrated Information Security Analysis Team Handoffs

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST 800-53 IR-10 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 IR-10 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.14.51: Integrated Information Security Analysis Team Handoffs.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.14.51' running on snow:incident with assignment_group transitions, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:incident category="Security" earliest=-30d
| transaction number maxspan=24h startswith="assignment_group=SOC" endswith="assignment_group=CTI"
| where duration>3600
| table number, duration, assignment_group, short_description
description: Tracks cross-team comments and assignments between SOC and CTI for IR-10 integrated infosec analysis team collaboration.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm and sourcetypes snow:incident. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Validate `transaction` performance — limit indexes; (2) alternatively use `streamstats` on sorted events; (3) KPI for handoff latency; (4) monthly leadership review. Indexes required: `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=snow:incident`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `snow:incident` with assignment_group transitions.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Validate `transaction` performance — limit indexes; (2) alternatively use `streamstats` on sorted events; (3) KPI for handoff latency; (4) monthly leadership review.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:incident category="Security" earliest=-30d
| transaction number maxspan=24h startswith="assignment_group=SOC" endswith="assignment_group=CTI"
| where duration>3600
| table number, duration, assignment_group, short_description
```

Understanding this SPL

**Integrated Information Security Analysis Team Handoffs (IR-10)** — Tracks cross-team comments and assignments between SOC and CTI for IR-10 integrated infosec analysis team collaboration.

Documented **Data sources**: `snow:incident` with assignment_group transitions. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:incident. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype=snow:incident, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Groups related events into transactions — prefer `maxspan`/`maxpause`/`maxevents` for bounded memory.
• Filters the current rows with `where duration>3600` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: assignment_group, duration, number, short_description.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (slow handoffs), Time chart (median duration)
grandmaExplanation: We watch for tracks cross-team comments and assignments between soc and cti for integrated infosec analysis team collaboration. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
